{"body":"Fix required before merging latest EC info in #32210:

```
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has an xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
```","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGWzhsQ","author":{"login":"balhoff"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please investigate and propose what to do. Is there interaction with #32207? If so maybe the fix needs to be in the same PR.","createdAt":"2026-06-16T17:36:03Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721533361","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGWzqwA","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27636312923)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-16T17:36:24Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721535680","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGW1TVw","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"## Investigation

Yes — direct interaction with #32207. Both errors come from the same upstream change and the same GO term, and the fix already in PR #32211 (which addresses #32207) resolves both errors here as well.

### Why the two issues are one fix

EC:1.14.13.43 and RHEA:10836 were obsoleted in lockstep upstream — the EC entry reads `Transferred entry: 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96` and Rhea split RHEA:10836 into RHEA:86147 + RHEA:86143. GO:0050246 (`questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`) is the only GO term carrying either of those obsolete IDs.

PR #32211 obsoletes GO:0050246 and mints two replacements (GO:7770080 reductase, GO:7770081 dioxygenase) carrying the new EC and RHEA IDs. As part of that obsoletion the diff strips both:

```
-xref: EC:1.14.13.43 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
-xref: RHEA:10836 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
```

### Both errors silenced — including the synonym-xref one

The QC rule at `src/util/ontology-qc.dl:80`:

```
error("... has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: ...") :- synonym_xref(term, _, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
```

is guarded by `valid_term`, which is defined (line 54) as `term(iri), !obsolete_term(iri)`. Once PR #32211 sets `is_obsolete: true` on GO:0050246, it drops out of `valid_term` and the rule no longer fires — so the two surviving `synonym ... RELATED [EC:1.14.13.43]` lines on the obsoleted term (kept intentionally, since they document what the synonyms came from) do **not** re-trigger this error. Same goes for line 76 (the plain xref error), which is already moot once the `xref: EC:1.14.13.43` line is removed.

### Recommendation

No separate PR or follow-on commit needed. #32211 already lands the fix for both #32207 and #32212. Suggest:

- Hold this issue open as a tracker, close it on merge of #32211 (or mark it a duplicate of #32207).
- After #32211 merges, the periodic `refresh-obsolete-ec` PR (#32210) should be able to land cleanly — same way the periodic Rhea refresh (#32203) will.

If you'd prefer I amend #32211 to explicitly mention #32212 in the description (so the merge auto-closes both), happy to do that.

---

- Triggered by: @balhoff
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27636312923)","createdAt":"2026-06-16T17:39:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721562455","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGW8wGA","author":{"login":"balhoff"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent #32211 can't be merged due to this error reported in its QC check:

```
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770080> has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.1.1.443"
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_7770081> has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.13.11.96"
```","createdAt":"2026-06-16T17:53:11Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721684504","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGXFf4A","author":{"login":"balhoff"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please respond to my comment above with a plan for getting #32211 merged.","createdAt":"2026-06-16T18:06:54Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721827808","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGXFydg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27637975967)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-16T18:07:28Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32212#issuecomment-4721832566","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGXIGTA","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"## Plan

The two errors are real and the fix is straightforward: **bundle the refreshed `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt` from #32210 into PR #32211**. The two PRs are currently mutually blocking, and merging them as one diff resolves both.

### Why the unknown-EC errors fire

`src/util/ontology-qc.dl:77,79` raises *unknown EC* whenever a term carries an `EC:` xref that isn't present in `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt`. That file is the cached output of a SPARQL query against Expasy's `enzyme.rdf` (Makefile target `../resources/obsolete_ec.txt`, refreshed weekly by `.github/workflows/refresh_obsolete_ec_file.yml`). The currently-committed copy on master predates the EC release that introduced 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96, so the QC sees them as "unknown" rather than "valid".

### Why this is the same fix the periodic refresh is trying to land

PR #32210 (`Refresh obsolete ECs file`, branch `refresh-obsolete-ecs`) is the scheduled weekly PR. Its diff against master:

- adds `EC:1.1.1.443	false`
- adds `EC:1.13.11.96	false`
- flips `EC:1.14.13.43` from `false` → `true`
- (plus ~10 other unrelated new ECs across the file)

And PR #32210 itself is failing QC for the **same** reason that originally opened this issue:

```
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has an xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
ERROR: <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0050246> has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: "EC:1.14.13.43"
```

So we have a chicken-and-egg: #32210 needs #32211 to obsolete GO:0050246 first; #32211 needs #32210 to teach the QC about EC:1.1.1.443 and EC:1.13.11.96. Neither can land alone.

### Why bundling is safe

Once both diffs are on the same branch:

- `GO:0050246` becomes `is_obsolete: true`, so `valid_term` no longer holds for it and the `obsolete EC` rules (`ontology-qc.dl:76,78,80`) don't fire on the legacy `EC:1.14.13.43` references — exactly the mechanism described in my earlier comment.
- The two new EC ids are present in `obsolete_ec.txt` with `false`, so the `unknown EC` rules (`ontology-qc.dl:77,79`) don't fire on `GO:7770080` / `GO:7770081`.
- The unrelated ~10 other new EC rows in #32210's diff are pure additions to a reference file and don't affect any other QC rule — same content the scheduled job would land regardless.

### Proposed action

1. On the `dragon-ai-agent-issue-32207-run8885` branch (PR #32211), copy `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt` from `refresh-obsolete-ecs` (PR #32210) and commit it. Single extra commit, two files touched in the PR (`go-edit.obo` + `obsolete_ec.txt`).
2. Re-run CI on #32211 — both `unknown EC` errors should clear, and the `obsolete EC` errors on GO:0050246 stay suppressed by the obsoletion.
3. After #32211 merges, close #32210 as superseded (the next weekly run on Monday will re-open it cleanly if Expasy ships anything new in the interim).
4. Close this issue (#32212) on merge of #32211, since the same diff resolves it.

If you want me to go ahead and apply step 1 now, say the word and I'll push the commit to the existing PR branch.

---

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Refresh src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt to include new EC ids

Bundles the refreshed obsolete_ec.txt from PR #32210 so the QC check
recognises EC:1.1.1.443 and EC:1.13.11.96 as valid (not unknown). The
two ids are the replacements minted by Rhea for the split of
RHEA:10836, and are xref'd from the new GO:7770080 (reductase) and
GO:7770081 (dioxygenase) terms added in this PR.

Without this refresh, PR CI fails with:
  ERROR: GO_7770080 has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.1.1.443"
  ERROR: GO_7770081 has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.13.11.96"

This resolves issue #32212 (obsolete EC errors are also suppressed
once GO:0050246 is obsoleted, as the relevant QC rules are guarded by
valid_term).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr comment 32211 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Added commit `df4aead`: bundled the refreshed `src/resources/obsolete_ec.txt` from #32210 so the QC recognises `EC:1.1.1.443` and `EC:1.13.11.96` as valid. Without this, PR CI was reporting:

```
ERROR: GO_7770080 has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.1.1.443"
ERROR: GO_7770081 has an xref to an unknown EC: "EC:1.13.11.96"
```

The diff against master is the same content the periodic refresh job (#32210) was trying to land — adds the two new EC ids, plus ~10 unrelated new rows for other ECs released in the same Expasy batch, and flips `EC:1.14.13.43` to obsolete. The obsoletion of GO:0050246 in this PR keeps the related #32212 errors suppressed (the `obsolete EC` QC rules are guarded by `valid_term`, so they don't fire on the obsoleted source term).

After this merges, #32210 can be closed as superseded — the next weekly run will re-open it cleanly if Expasy ships anything new in the interim. Resolves #32212 (and #32207 as before).

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @balhoff
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27639765278)
EOF
)"
